key_points: Incoming freshmen have very limited housing choice options
Dorm rooms are small and bland but can be decorated
Moving in takes far longer than expected; bring help
key_quotes: I would talk to someone at the school about that. Chances are as an incoming freshman you won't have as many options as you think you do.
The bikes don't move, every time I go by Scott Hall they are always there, no matter what time of the day. I think they're just decoration.
I'm glad to have had an opportunity like this 2x and then moving off campus.

key_points: Campus vibe described as welcoming and smaller community feel
Limited social scene data; no specific party or Greek life mentions
Bike infrastructure may exist in name only on campus
key_quotes: The vibe was welcoming.
For those who commented that they are going to USM I'm curious how was your experience!!
Since school bout to start I'm so addicted on watching back to school videos.

key_points: No specific dining hall reviews found in data
Students working part-time to cover food costs is common
International students explicitly warned food and rent costs add up fast
key_quotes: I pay for food and rent working 20 hours a week. But start applying for jobs before you come here.
I wouldn't recommend unless you eventually want to work or live here.
There's a reason that movies show students doing nothing but studying.

key_points: No direct mental health support reviews found in data
Financial stress is a recurring theme among students
International students especially report stress balancing work and studies
key_quotes: Study IS a full time job, remember, and it can be a lot harder than your prior education.
I would go for the option where you're not having to work.
I think it would be far worse for you to just go with it and end up wasting money on a degree you don't want.

key_points: USM is one of the few near-safety schools offering close to full aid for internationals
National Merit scholars can get strong scholarship packages here
International students still need to work to cover living expenses even with full tuition
key_quotes: Only Southern Miss is really close to what I would call a safety for an international student who needs full aid.
In USM, I got full tuition scholarship for 4 years so I would have to work to cover rent and food expenses only.
A 34 ACT and 3.9+ unweighted GPA gets merit award at Southern Miss.

key_points: Programs in nursing, CS, and polymer engineering mentioned positively
Online degree selection described as limited by a transfer student
Library Science program specifically flagged as a concern for job outcomes
key_quotes: The selection of online degrees there is rather limited.
Just as good program as other college.
I reccommend Southern Miss. You don't want to lock yourself into a major if you're not sure.

key_points: Deadline communication is inconsistent — website vs. email give different dates
Admissions office slow to respond during peak application periods
Low karma barrier blocks students from posting in school's own subreddit
key_quotes: The website said to have it received and reviewed by March 1st but the email they sent me said by February 23rd.
They must be knee deep in applications these days so a bit of a delay should be expected.
I wanted to ask this in r/SouthernMiss but didn't have enough Karma.

key_points: Campus is visually attractive and has improved significantly over decades
Hattiesburg crime rate is a serious concern raised by observers
Campus feels smaller and more manageable than large flagship schools
key_quotes: Sadly this university is practically dying. Crime in Hattiesburg is so bad, I can't see why anyone would let their kid live and invest their education in this place.
I didn't realize how lovely it is.
I went to USM in the '80s and it was great seeing this video. It has changed a lot for the better.

key_points: CS degree value questioned by prospective international students
Nursing grads from lower-tier schools earn same as elite school grads
Degree prestige concern is real — students warned about perception issues
key_quotes: I saw this one month old post saying that doing bachelors from a 90% acceptance rate uni is like destroying your career.
Where you go to nursing school legit doesn't matter even one bit.
don't worry about 'destroying your career' that's just this sub being dramatic.

key_points: Extremely strong value for scholarship recipients and international students
Cheaper tuition versus comparable programs at larger schools is a key draw
Full tuition still leaves students needing to work for living expenses
key_quotes: Cheaper tuition, just as good program as other college.
Full tuition scholarship for 4 years so I would have to work to cover rent and food expenses only.
I reccommend Southern Miss. You don't want to lock yourself into a major if you're not sure, nor do you want to get yourself in any more debt than you need to.

key_points: Welcoming small-campus feel appeals to those who know what they want
Strong draw for budget-conscious and international scholarship seekers
Not a prestige school — students choose it for finances, not reputation
key_quotes: The vibe was welcoming.
I think the issue lots of international students don't ever consider these schools because of PRESTIGE.
as someone from hattiesburg whose parent is a faculty member I might be able to give you some insight into the culture.

key_points: **Enrollment decline:** enrollment has dropped 25% in 10 years per one commenter
**Graduation rate:** only ~30% graduation rate flagged as diploma mill concern
**Safety:** Hattiesburg crime described as bad enough to deter enrollment
**Reproductive rights:** one commenter warns about Mississippi abortion restrictions affecting students
key_quotes: Sadly this university is practically dying. Their enrollment has plummeted by 25% in the last 10 years - and their graduation rate is only around 30%. It's kinda a diploma mill at this point.
Crime in Hattiesburg is so bad, I can't see why anyone would let their kid live and invest their education in this place.
Just be careful, don't get pregnant or get anyone pregnant in Mississippi unless you are ok with forced birthing.

key_points: One of the only near-full-aid options for international students in the US
High-stat students can get strong merit awards most overlook due to prestige bias
Smaller campus means less competition for resources and more community feel
key_quotes: Only Southern Miss is really close to what I would call a safety for an international student who needs full aid.
A 34 ACT and 3.9+ unweighted GPA gets merit award at Southern Miss.
You don't want to lock yourself into a major if you're not sure, nor do you want to get yourself in any more debt than you need to.
